title: holoschoenus romanus subsp. holoschoenus
intro: Holoschoenus romanus subsp. holoschoenus is a plant species commonly known as Roman club rush, which belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Holoschoenus romanus subsp. holoschoenus include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to support wound healing and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
